title: Quick Tempo
explanation: Your downswing starts before the backswing finishes — the classic snatch. Rushed transitions throw the club off line and cost you the lag that creates speed.
drill: Count 'one-and-two' out loud: back on 'one-and', through on 'two'. Swing at 80% until the count feels boring.

title: Backswing Drift
explanation: Your backswing drifts so slowly that the downswing becomes a lunge to catch up. Great tempo is rhythmic, not slow.
drill: Swing to a metronome feel: brisk 'ONE-two' with no pause at the top. Let the club rebound naturally.

title: Head Sway
explanation: Your head slides sideways during the swing instead of staying centered. The swing's low point follows your head — sway is the #1 cause of fat and thin contact.
drill: Practice with your lead temple almost touching a wall or alignment stick. Turn around your spine; don't slide along it.

title: Hip Slide
explanation: Your hips slide laterally instead of rotating. Sliding stalls the body, dumps the club behind you, and forces the hands to save the strike.
drill: Set a chair just outside your lead hip. Turn to the finish without bumping it — rotate, don't slide.

title: Slouched Setup
explanation: Not enough forward tilt at address — standing too upright forces a flat, armsy swing and inconsistent contact.
drill: Hip-hinge drill: club along your spine, hinge forward until your chest points at the ball, soft knees.

title: Hunched Setup
explanation: Too much forward bend at address — a hunched spine can't rotate, so the swing lifts and chops.
drill: Stand tall, hinge from the hips only until your arms hang straight down under your shoulders. That's your tilt.

title: Collapsing Lead Arm
explanation: Your lead arm folds at the top, shrinking your arc — that's width and speed left on the table, and an extra moving part to time on the way down.
drill: L-to-L swings feeling the lead arm stay long (soft, not rigid). Width back = speed through.

title: Off-Balance Finish
explanation: You can't hold your finish — which means you weren't in control at impact either. Balance problems usually trace back to swinging beyond your tempo.
drill: Every practice swing ends in a 3-second statue: weight on the lead foot, trail toe down, chest at the target.

title: Club Steep at Delivery
explanation: Coming down, your hands are outside the plane they started on — the over-the-top move that produces pulls and slices.
drill: From the top, feel the club head drop BEHIND you before anything turns. Pump drill: top → drop → stop, three times, then swing.

title: Club Stuck Inside
explanation: The club drops too far under the plane coming down — 'stuck' — forcing flippy hands and blocks or snap hooks.
drill: Feel your chest keep turning through the shot so the club can swing OUT to the ball instead of getting trapped behind you.

title: Inside Takeaway
explanation: Your hands pull inside the plane line in the first three feet, which usually forces a steep re-route at the top.
drill: One-piece takeaway: chest turns the club back with the hands staying in front of you until the club passes your trail foot.

title: Early Extension
explanation: Your hips thrust toward the ball on the downswing, standing you up out of your posture — contact and start line go with it.
drill: Practice with your rear against a wall or chair: keep it touching from the top through impact.

title: Narrow Base
explanation: Your feet are closer than your shoulders at address. A narrow base costs balance, speed and stability — the three things the stance exists to provide.
drill: Use the routine: feet together at the ball, take your grip, then step out until the INSIDES of your feet sit under your shoulders.

title: Stance Too Wide
explanation: Wider than shoulders feels stable but locks the hips — you can't rotate or shift, so the arms take over.
drill: Feet together, grip, then one step out each side to shoulder width. Hit half swings until turning feels easy again.

title: Hanging Back
explanation: Your weight never made it to the lead side — the twist happened without the push. Hanging back is behind thin strikes, fat strikes and weak flights.
drill: Club across your shoulders: turn back, then unwind and PUSH into the lead foot until the trail foot rolls onto its tiptoe. Finish with 90% of you on the lead side, hold 3 seconds.
